food_subgroup: Brewed tea beverages with fruit flavouring

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general oolong tea beverage class (FOODON_03301103) but no specific entry for yuzu-flavoured oolong tea. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0LQ covers oolong tea as a category. No specific yuzu-oolong tea code exists; closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34728003 refers to oolong tea as a substance. No specific yuzu-flavoured oolong tea concept exists; closest parent used.

serving.description: 1 medium cup (approximately 500 ml at standard sweetness)
serving.natural_language_portion: One medium cup of Yuzu Oolong Tea as served at KOI Thé, approximately 500 ml, typically ordered at 50% or 100% sweetness with ice.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA SR Legacy 14355) adapted with yuzu juice contribution; sugar content estimated from KOI Thé standard sweetness formulation cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central yuzu juice entry
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for yuzu oolong tea exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy brewed oolong tea (FDC ID 14367) as the base, with a small yuzu juice contribution (USDA FDC ID 168155) and a sugar syrup addition estimated at 50% sweetness level (approximately 5 g sugar per 100 ml). This is a medium-confidence estimate. Values represent the ready-to-drink beverage as served.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened tea beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for yuzu oolong tea. GI estimated at 35 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened tea beverages and oolong tea analogues, which consistently fall in the low GI range (GI 30–45) due to the polyphenol content of oolong tea moderating glucose absorption. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 25.0) / 100 = 8.75, rounded to 8.8. Serving carbohydrate estimated at 5.0 g per 100 ml × 500 ml = 25.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; moderate potassium from tea and yuzu
hypertension.guidance: Yuzu oolong tea contains approximately 3 mg sodium per 100 ml and provides modest potassium (~20 mg/100 ml), making it a low-sodium beverage suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though sweetness level should be monitored to avoid excess caloric intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content variable by sweetness level; low GI base tea
type2_diabetes.guidance: At standard 50% sweetness, yuzu oolong tea contributes approximately 25 g of sugar per 500 ml serving (GL ~8.8), which is moderate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; ordering at 0% or 25% sweetness significantly reduces the glycaemic impact and is recommended for better blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat; oolong tea polyphenols with potential cardioprotective effects
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Yuzu oolong tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides oolong tea polyphenols (theaflavins and catechins) that have been associated with modest improvements in LDL cholesterol and endothelial function in peer-reviewed studies, making it a heart-friendly beverage choice when consumed at low or no added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yuzu Oolong Tea (Medium) is a Taiwanese-style cold brewed tea beverage sold at KOI Thé, a popular Taiwanese tea chain with outlets across Singapore. The drink combines oolong tea — a partially oxidised Chinese tea known for its floral, fruity, and slightly roasted character — with yuzu, a fragrant East Asian citrus fruit (Citrus junos) prized for its tart, aromatic juice that blends notes of lemon, grapefruit, and mandarin. The result is a refreshing, lightly sweet, citrus-forward tea with a smooth, mellow body and a clean finish. At KOI Thé, the medium size is approximately 500 ml and is served cold with ice. Customers can customise sweetness (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%) and ice level. At 50% sweetness, the beverage contains approximately 20 kcal per 100 ml, with 5 g of carbohydrate per 100 ml primarily from added sugar syrup. The tea base contributes minimal protein (0.1 g/100 ml) and negligible fat. Oolong tea is rich in polyphenols including theaflavins and catechins, which have been studied for antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potential cardioprotective effects. Yuzu contributes a small amount of vitamin C. The glycaemic index of the beverage is estimated at 35 (low GI), though the glycaemic load per 500 ml serving is approximately 8.8 at standard sweetness, which is moderate. Clinically, this beverage is suitable for most dietary patterns but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should opt for reduced sweetness levels. It is naturally dairy-free, gluten-free, vegan, and halal-friendly. The Chinese name 金柚乌龙茶 (jīn yòu wūlóng chá) translates literally as 'golden pomelo oolong tea', reflecting the golden colour of yuzu juice.
common_user_queries: How many calories in KOI Yuzu Oolong Tea?
Is KOI tea suitable for diabetics?
Is yuzu oolong tea dairy free?
How much sugar is in KOI medium tea?
Yuzu oolong tea GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient entry for yuzu oolong tea exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy brewed oolong tea with yuzu juice contribution and estimated sugar addition at 50% sweetness. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened tea beverages. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
notes: 1. Fat_g is confirmed zero (0.0) for this beverage as oolong tea and yuzu juice contain no fat by nature; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central oolong tea and yuzu juice entries. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero for the same reason. 2. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as the beverage is a liquid infusion with no fibre-containing solids. 3. Protein_g is 0.1 g per 100 ml, a trace amount from the tea infusion; not zero. 4. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all set to -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA SR Legacy oolong tea entry or the yuzu juice entry at a level that can be reliably attributed to the ready-to-drink beverage; values are not reported in the source database for this beverage form. 5. Glycemic_index set to 35 (low GI) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sweetened tea beverage entries; no direct GI measurement exists for yuzu oolong tea. Glycemic_load_per_serving calculated at 8.8 for a 500 ml medium serving at 50% sweetness (25 g carbohydrate × GI 35 / 100). 6. Nutrient values represent the beverage at 50% sweetness as a reference point; actual values will vary with sweetness customisation. 7. Price SGD 5.10 preserved from input (field survey data, KOI Thé Funan). 8. This record covers the medium (中) size as indicated by the input food_name prefix 'M-'.
